single_43430_20260709_095938.question_text: Will Pope Leo XIV visit another country other than Spain before September 2026?
single_43430_20260709_095938.refresh_reason: manual single refresh (community prediction shift alert)
single_43430_20260709_095938.reasoning:
(a) **Time left until resolution:** Resolution date is September 1, 2026. Today is July 9, 2026. Approximately 7-8 weeks remain.

(b) **Status quo outcome if nothing changes:** The question has already partially resolved — according to the research, Pope Leo XIV has already visited multiple countries other than Spain (Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, Equatorial Guinea on the Africa tour, April 13-23, 2026; and Turkey/Lebanon in late 2025). However, the resolution criteria specifies visits **after May 18, 2026** and before September 1, 2026. The Africa tour was April 13-23, which is BEFORE May 18, 2026. The Spain visit is June 6-12, 2026, which is after May 18. I need to check whether any non-Spain country visit occurred after May 18, 2026.

(c) **Scenario for NO outcome:** The Africa tour (April 13-23) falls before the May 18 cutoff. The Spain visit is explicitly excluded. No other country visit occurs after May 18 and before September 1, 2026.

(d) **Scenario for YES outcome:** Pope Leo XIV visits any UN member state other than Spain or Italy after May 18, 2026, and before September 1, 2026. Given the Spain trip runs June 6-12, it's possible he combines it with a nearby country visit, or makes a separate trip in July-August 2026.

(e) **Base rate:** No reliable base rate available — proceeding on priors. However, modern popes have been quite active travelers. Given that Leo XIV has already completed one Africa tour and a Turkey/Lebanon trip in his first year, he appears to be a frequent traveler. The background mentions Monaco as a previous visit too.

(f) **How the live data/research moves me:**

Key analysis of the resolution criteria: The question asks about visits **after May 18, 2026** and before September 1, 2026. 

- Africa tour: April 13-23, 2026 — **before** May 18, so does NOT count.
- Spain: June 6-12, 2026 — explicitly excluded by resolution criteria.
- Monaco: Listed in background as previously visited — need to check timing. The background says he "has visited Turkey, Lebanon, Monaco, Algeria, Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea." Monaco's visit timing is unclear but likely also before May 18.

The question was originally forecast at 18%, suggesting forecasters thought this was unlikely. However, today is July 9 — already past the Spain visit and more than halfway through the resolution window. 

The research doesn't explicitly mention any confirmed trip after May 18 other than Spain. However, given Leo XIV's active travel schedule (7+ countries in his first year), the probability of at least one more non-Spain foreign trip between May 18 and September 1 seems moderate-to-high. That's a ~3.5-month window for an active papal traveler.

The Spain trip runs June 6-12. After that, there are roughly 2.5 months (mid-June to end of August) where another trip could occur. Popes often avoid major travel in August (Vatican traditionally has a summer pause), but July is viable.

The fact that the research mentions an invitation to Mexico "on the table" and references no plans to visit the US in 2026 suggests some travel planning is ongoing. With Leo XIV's demonstrated willingness to travel frequently, and July being a plausible travel month, I'd estimate a meaningful probability.

(g) **How my view has changed:** The original 18% forecast likely assumed fewer confirmed trips. The research confirms he's been very active but the Africa trip falls before the cutoff. The question is now essentially: will he make another international trip (non-Spain) in the ~10-week window after May 18? Given his travel pace and the July window remaining, I'd put this significantly higher than 18%.

(h) **Community prediction:** Not explicitly provided, but the question notes a "community prediction shift alert" prompted this refresh from 18%. Given the active travel schedule and the remaining window still open (July 9 to September 1), I believe the probability is substantially higher than 18%.

We're already in July, and if no trip had been announced or taken yet post-Spain, this would lower confidence. But the research doesn't confirm definitively that no such trip has occurred or been announced for July-August. Given the active travel pace and remaining ~7 weeks, I'd estimate around 55-65%.

Probability: 62%
single_43430_20260709_095938.research_text:
I've reviewed the summary carefully for internal contradictions regarding metrics, counts, dates, and time periods.

**Finding:** There is one contradiction regarding the number of nations visited during the Africa tour.

The summary states: "He has already completed an 11-day, four-nation tour of Africa from April 13-23, 2026, visiting Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea"

However, the summary then lists only three nations confirmed with specific evidence:
- Algeria (confirmed by Cardinal Jean-Paul Vesco)
- Angola (confirmed by Archbishop Kryspin Dubiel on January 13, 2026)
- Turkey and Lebanon (previous trip in November-December 2025)

Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea are mentioned in the opening sentence but lack supporting citations or confirmation in the rest of the summary.

**Revised Summary:**

Yes, Pope Leo XIV will visit countries other than Spain before September 2026. He has already completed an Africa tour from April 13-23, 2026, visiting Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea [AP News](https://apnews.com/article/pope-leo-xiv-concludes-africa-visit-04-23-2026), [Vatican.va](https://press.vatican.va/content/salastampa/en/bollettino/pubblico/2026/04/22/260422a.html).

Angola was explicitly confirmed as a destination on January 13, 2026, with the Vatican's envoy, Archbishop Kryspin Dubiel, stating the Pope had accepted President Joao Lourenco's invitation [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/pope-leo-visit-angola-part-an-africa-tour-vatican-envoy-says-2026-01-13/). Cardinal Jean-Paul Vesco of Algiers confirmed a papal visit to Algeria was "on track" in early January and would occur in the "first part of 2026" [OSV News](https://www.osvnews.com/algeria-angola-spain-among-2026-papal-trips-with-invitation-to-mexico-on-the-table/), [USCCB](https://www.usccb.org/news/2026/pope-has-no-plans-travel-us-2026-vatican-says).

Pope Leo XIV also expressed a wish to visit Algeria, the birthplace of St. Augustine of Hippo [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/pope-leo-visit-angola-part-an-africa-tour-vatican-envoy-says-2026-01-13/), [OSV News](https://www.osvnews.com/algeria-angola-spain-among-2026-papal-trips-with-invitation-to-mexico-on-the-table/). His only previous overseas trip was to Turkey and Lebanon in November and December 2025 [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/pope-leo-visit-angola-part-an-africa-tour-vatican-envoy-says-2026
